description: Tier-3 warm-pool latency + throughput + scaling. Persistent NRP worker pods (queue-group subscribers) over the leaf bridge (real NRP<->Atlas WAN); driver on the Atlas hub.

setup.workers: ignored-range CPU Job pods (cpu=1, mem=2Gi), queue-group subscribers on subject bench.work, bounded lifetime, auto-cleaned
setup.policy: <=4 pods, ignored range, exit-0, ttl + delete cleanup

finding: Warm-pool per-task latency ~67 ms (WAN-RTT bound) vs ~6.6 s ephemeral-Job cold-start -- the pool removes the ~100x cold-start penalty. Throughput plateaus ~930/s at >=2 workers because for trivial I/O-bound tasks the ceiling is driver_concurrency / RTT (Little's law: 64 / 0.067 ~ 955/s), not worker count. Worker-count scaling matters for compute-bound tasks; here 1-2 workers already saturate the link. This also gives the burst-path-overhead contrast: warm pool ~67 ms vs cold Job ~6.6 s.
